what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment, linking an attachment ID to an image target in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target points to a presentation-15p image at page/slide p-02
Serves as a ledger-style metadata entry
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Attachment IDs use the att- prefix
Channel-memory attachments are stored as individual markdown files
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
concerns: Target URI is opaque with no resolution context in the file
No title, caption, or description for the attachment
No MIME type, dimensions, or file size
notes: A minimal ledger entry that maps a design-source attachment ID to a specific presentation image slide.
questions: Does p-02 denote a slide, page, or asset variant?
What is the relationship between this attachment and a specific channel message or design artifact?
Who added this attachment and what triggered it?
intention_signals: To catalog and timestamp an image attachment in the design-source channel so the associated presentation image can be referenced and traced.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped identifier and a target URI for an image attachment in the design-source channel, which a fused system can use to locate and reference the associated presentation image.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata file att-fce696fb in the design-source channel records an images attachment targeting image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-02 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register and timestamp an image attachment reference for a design-source presentation image.
